specific_mechanism: KCOT: Krylov Chain-of-Thought. Define LLM 'reasoning operator' A_θ (k-th forward pass through CoT prompt). Initial residual r_0 = embed(question). Generate Krylov basis r_k = A_θ r_{k-1} - α_k Σ_{j<k} β_{jk} r_j (Gram-Schmidt against previous basis vectors). Each r_k decodes to one CoT step. Phase-coherent: basis vectors maintain orthogonality in A-induced inner product (CG conjugacy). Stop when ‖r_n‖ < ε.
llm_application: KCOT (Krylov Chain-of-Thought): The LLM acts as the operator A_θ. Start r_0 = question embedding. For k=1..n: (1) generate candidate next step v_k = LLM(prompt with r_{k-1} as context); (2) orthogonalize embed(v_k) against {r_0,..,r_{k-1}} via Gram-Schmidt under embedding inner product; (3) decode the orthogonalized r_k back to natural-language step; (4) compute residual norm ‖proj_{remaining-problem}(r_k)‖. Stop when residual < ε or n=n_max. The CoT scratchpad is the orthogonal Krylov basis.
novelty_claim: Multiple iterative-refinement CoT methods exist (Progressive Thought Refinement; Iteration-of-Thought; Self-Refine) but none uses Krylov-style basis orthogonalization as the algorithmic backbone with convergence-bound stopping.
motivation_justification: Krylov subspace methods are the canonical iterative residual-minimization framework. The candidate transfers the exact algorithm (basis generation + Gram-Schmidt + residual stopping) to CoT scratchpad construction.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: LLM as reasoning operator A_θ
M_2: Krylov basis generation via Gram-Schmidt
content_words: reasoning operator forward pass orthogonal step
monotone error reduction transformer CoT step

llm_side: reasoning operator forward pass orthogonal step
monotone error reduction transformer CoT step
